summary: Ukraine received an IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher and reported over 2,300 hostile drones, guided bombs and rockets launched by Russia in the past week. Russian forces continue extensive Shahed‑type UAV attacks across northern and eastern regions, with Ukrainian air defences claiming to have downed 212 hostile drones. Diplomatic tensions rise as Ukraine warns neighboring states and identifies 500 potential targets in Belarus.
key_points: Ukraine received an IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher, enhancing its missile defence capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Russian forces launched more than 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets against Ukrainian positions in the last week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Ukrainian air defences reported shooting down 212 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones across the north and east (Source: @kpszsu/63369).
Ukraine identified the first 500 potential strike targets inside Belarus, warning Lukashenko against joining the war (Source: @UkraineNow/81576).

Multiple Shahed UAV groups were observed approaching from the south, north and east across Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Rivne and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Sources: @eRadarrua/94474, @kpszsu/63363, @kpszsu/63368, @kpszsu/63401, @kpszsu/63370).
A Ukrainian strike hit a school in Vasylivka, Zaporizhzhia, damaging a bus and building but causing no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/378319).
Russia claimed a Ukrainian drone struck the occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant; Ukraine dismissed the claim as misinformation (Source: @UkraineNow/81565).

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine received an IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher while reporting over 2,300 hostile drones, guided bombs and rockets launched by Russia in the past week. Ukrainian forces claim to have downed 212 enemy UAVs across the north and east, but Russian Shahed attacks persist. Diplomatic warnings target Belarus with 500 identified strike points.

points: President Zelensky announced the delivery of an IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher, stating it will improve protection for civilians and reduce Russian aerial advantage (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).

The Ukrainian Ministry of Foreign Affairs rejected Russian accusations of a Ukrainian drone attack on the occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, labeling them an information operation (Source: @UkraineNow/81565).
Ukrainian forces reported a strike on a school in Vasylivka, Zaporizhzhia, damaging a bus and the school building without casualties (Source: @tass_agency/378319).
A Ukrainian drone allegedly destroyed a Nova Poshta post office in Dnipro, causing the building to burn completely; no injuries were reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Russian officials claimed Ukrainian forces attacked the occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant with a drone, though no evidence was provided (Source: @UkraineNow/81565).
Russia threatened neighboring countries, urging European support for Armenia, Moldova, the Baltic states, Azerbaijan and Georgia (Source: @UkraineNow/81558).
Russian media reported that the EU is considering temporary suspension of price caps on Russian oil due to the Iran conflict (Source: @tass_agency/378308).
Russia announced a high‑profile attack on a school of boxing in Enerhodar, damaging windows and a bus (Source: @tass_agency/378330).

Multiple Shahed‑type UAV groups were observed moving from the south toward Kharkiv and Sumy regions, including three UAVs along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border heading to Krasnokutsk (Source: @eRadarrua/94474).
Ukrainian air defences reported 212 enemy UAVs downed across the north and east, with 14 strike drones hitting 11 locations, including in the Donetsk and Luhansk areas (Source: @kpszsu/63369).
A Shahed UAV group from Bryansk region entered Sumy and Chernihiv oblasts, indicating cross‑border aerial pressure from Russia (Source: @kpszsu/63363).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a school in Vasylivka, Zaporizhzhia, part of the broader eastern operational area (Source: @tass_agency/378319).
Ukrainian radar observed Shahed UAVs approaching from the north along the Belarus border in Chernihiv and Kyiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
UAV groups were reported over Rivne and Zhytomyr from the north, heading toward Rivne and Ovruch respectively (Source: @kpszsu/63368).
Two Shahed drones were reported over Dnipro, with additional UAVs detected over Zaporizhzhia from the south (Source: @kpszsu/63363).
Ukrainian forces claimed 212 hostile UAVs were shot down across the north, confirming intense aerial activity in this sector (Source: @kpszsu/63369).
A Ukrainian drone attack in occupied Kherson region hit a fuel tanker in Sal'kovo, preventing fuel delivery to Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/94476).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a logistics warehouse and vehicles in Dnipro, causing fires and potential casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81569).
Multiple UAV groups were observed moving from the south into Dnipropetrovsk region, including attacks on Nikopol district (Source: @kpszsu/63412).
Ukrainian air defences reported downing UAVs over the southern front, contributing to the overall 212 UAVs destroyed tally (Source: @kpszsu/63369).
Ukrainian air defence forces reported shooting down 212 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation types, across northern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63369).
Russian forces launched over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets in the past week, indicating a high‑intensity aerial campaign (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Ukrainian radar detected multiple UAV groups approaching from Russia's Bryansk and Belgorod regions toward Sumy, Chernihiv and Kharkiv (Sources: @kpszsu/63363, @kpszsu/63370).
Ukrainian forces claimed a successful strike on a school in Vasylivka, Zaporizhzhia, demonstrating continued UAV offensive capability (Source: @tass_agency/378319).

key_developments: Ukraine received an IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher, enhancing its missile defence capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Russian forces launched more than 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets against Ukrainian positions in the last week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Ukrainian air defences reported shooting down 212 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones across the north and east (Source: @kpszsu/63369).
Ukraine identified the first 500 potential strike targets inside Belarus, warning Lukashenko against joining the war (Source: @UkraineNow/81576).

Multiple Shahed UAV groups were observed approaching from the south, north and east across Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Rivne and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Sources: @eRadarrua/94474, @kpszsu/63363, @kpszsu/63368, @kpszsu/63401, @kpszsu/63370).
A Ukrainian strike hit a school in Vasylivka, Zaporizhzhia, damaging a bus and building but causing no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/378319).

sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial pressure remains intense, with large‑scale UAV and missile launches targeting northern, eastern and southern fronts. Ukrainian air defences have shown resilience, downing over 200 hostile drones, yet the volume of attacks suggests Russia seeks to overwhelm defenses. The identification of 500 Belarusian targets indicates a potential expansion of the conflict zone, raising the risk of broader regional escalation.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering its air defence through foreign aid, notably the IRIS‑T system from Germany, while urging neighboring states to resist Russian influence. Russia continues to threaten adjacent countries, seeking to isolate Ukraine diplomatically. International observers note EU considerations on Russian oil pricing and heightened attention to the Zaporizhzhia nuclear facility.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV activity patterns show coordinated groups from Russian border regions targeting key logistics and infrastructure nodes across multiple oblasts. The high number of downed drones suggests effective Ukrainian radar and missile coverage but also highlights the saturation tactics employed by Russia. Monitoring of Belarusian target lists and potential missile launches from Bryansk warrants heightened vigilance.
